senses.definition: students as a whole that are taught together

examples.x: The teacher greeted the class as they entered the classroom, ready to begin the day's lesson.
The class participated eagerly in the group discussion, sharing their diverse perspectives on the topic.
Every Friday, the class gathers for a weekly quiz to test their understanding of the material covered during the week.
The class enjoyed a field trip to the museum, where they explored exhibits related to their history curriculum.
The class elected a representative to voice their concerns and suggestions during student council meetings.

senses.definition: a specific group of students who progress through their studies together over the course of an academic period

examples.x: The graduating year organized a memorable farewell event to commemorate their time together.
The incoming year eagerly awaited orientation to meet their new classmates and faculty.
The senior year faced the challenges of college applications and final exams as they approached graduation.

senses.definition: a group of students within a British school who are organized together based on factors such as age or academic ability

examples.x: The students in form 10B participated in a science fair competition.
The form 8 students worked together on a group project for their history class.
The school divided the students into different forms to better accommodate their learning needs.
Form representatives were elected to voice the concerns of their classmates during student council meetings.
Each form competed in various sports events during the school's annual sports day.

senses.definition: a distinct part or subdivision of a larger group of learners

examples.x: In our school, each grade is divided into several sections, with about twenty students in each.
The teacher organized the students into sections based on their proficiency levels in French.
Students from different sections often collaborate on projects and assignments together.
The school administration assigns classrooms based on the availability of sections and teachers.
The competition was fierce among the sections to achieve the highest scores in the science fair.

senses.definition: a group of students who are taught together in the same class or classes, typically based on their academic ability or chosen subjects
a social club for female students in a university or college, especially in the US and Canada

examples.x: Joining a sorority can provide students with opportunities for leadership, friendship, and community service.
Sororities often organize social events, philanthropic activities, and academic support programs for their members.
Membership in a sorority can create lifelong bonds and connections with other women who share similar values and interests.
Sorority houses serve as hubs for social gatherings and group activities, fostering a sense of belonging among members.
Sorority recruitment is a competitive process where potential new members visit different chapters to find the one that best fits their personality and goals.

senses.definition: a social club for male students in a university or college, especially in the US and Canada

examples.x: He pledged to join a fraternity during his freshman year at university.
The fraternity hosted a charity event to raise funds for a local cause.
Members of the fraternity often lived together in a shared house near campus.
The fraternity held regular social events, including parties and barbecues.
He formed lifelong friendships through his involvement in the fraternity during his college years.

senses.definition: a group formed by students within an educational institution to pursue common interests, activities, or goals outside of the formal curriculum

examples.x: The student society organized a fundraiser to support a local charity.
As a member of the student society, Sarah participated in community service projects throughout the year.
The student society hosted a film screening followed by a discussion on social issues.
John joined the student society to connect with peers who shared his passion for entrepreneurship.
The student society elected new officers to lead initiatives and events for the upcoming semester.

senses.definition: a representative body composed of students elected by their peers to advocate for student interests, plan school events, and address student concerns
a group of people who meet regularly to discuss books that they have read together
a small gathering of students who come together to review course material, discuss concepts, and prepare for exams or assignments collectively
a group of students who progress through an academic program together, often sharing classes and experiences
the main sports teams representing a college or university in intercollegiate competitions

examples.x: The varsity football team had a successful season, winning the championship.
She earned a spot on the varsity basketball team after impressing the coach during tryouts.
The varsity athletes trained rigorously to prepare for the upcoming match against their rival school.
Varsity sports are an integral part of campus life, drawing large crowds to games and events.

senses.definition: the teacher student conventions that are held in the morning or afternoon in the school
a collaborative organization formed by parents and teachers to support educational activities and student well-being within a school community

examples.x: As a member of the parent-teacher association, Sarah volunteered to help coordinate the school's annual book fair.

senses.definition: a representative group that addresses school-related issues and initiatives, consisting of students, teachers, and sometimes parents or community members
a governing body wit
the staff who teach or conduct research in a university or college

examples.x: The faculty held a meeting to discuss student performance.
The new faculty include experts in various scientific fields.
The faculty at this institution is highly respected in their field.
He joined the faculty after completing his postdoctoral research.
The faculty were pleased with the students' progress.

senses.definition: an American organization that creates tests for students aiming for college and provides resources to help them plan for higher education
an organization in the United States that recognizes and promotes academic excellence and achievement among its members

examples.x: She was thrilled to receive an invitation to join the honor society in recognition of her outstanding grades and leadership skills.
Membership in the honor society provided opportunities for networking and professional development beyond the classroom.
The honor society held regular meetings and events to foster camaraderie among its members and promote academic success.
Graduating with honors and being inducted into the honor society were among her proudest accomplishments.
The honor society awarded scholarships to deserving students who demonstrated academic excellence and community involvement.

senses.definition: an academic honor society that recognizes and celebrates excellence in the liberal arts and sciences

examples.x: She was inducted into Phi Beta Kappa for her outstanding academic achievements.
The university's Phi Beta Kappa chapter holds regular events to promote scholarship and intellectual exchange.
Only the top students in the graduating class are invited to join Phi Beta Kappa.
His Phi Beta Kappa key proudly displayed on his bookshelf symbolized his dedication to academic excellence.
Membership in Phi Beta Kappa is considered a prestigious honor among students and alumni alike.

senses.definition: the community of scholars and educators engaged in higher education and research

examples.x: She aspired to pursue a career in academia and become a professor of literature.
The conference brought together leading scholars from academia to discuss advancements in medical research.
Academia often values intellectual curiosity and critical thinking skills.
The book provides insights into the challenges and rewards of navigating the competitive landscape of academia.
In academia, collaboration between different disciplines can lead to innovative solutions to complex problems.
